criteria.name: Awards
criteria.evidence: First prize in two international architecture competitions
criteria.rationale: Two first-place wins in international competitions constitute nationally/internationally recognized prizes in the field.

criteria.name: Membership
criteria.rationale: No membership in associations requiring outstanding achievement was described.

criteria.name: Press
criteria.evidence: Coverage in Dezeen and Architectural Record
criteria.rationale: Both are major trade publications with international reach, constituting published material about the applicant in professional media.

criteria.name: Judging
criteria.rationale: Peer review of others' work qualifies as judging, but a single journal with no detail on frequency or standing is thin.

criteria.name: Original contribution
criteria.evidence: Published monograph on design practice; led design of three landmark civic buildings
criteria.rationale: Landmark civic projects may reflect original contributions of major significance, but no specific innovation, influence on the field, or adoption by others was described.

criteria.name: Scholarly articles
criteria.evidence: Authored a published monograph on design practice
criteria.rationale: A monograph can qualify, but no peer-reviewed journal articles were mentioned and the monograph's publisher and scholarly standing are unspecified.

criteria.name: Artistic exhibitions
criteria.evidence: Work exhibited at the Venice Architecture Biennale and a solo gallery show
criteria.rationale: The Venice Architecture Biennale is a premier international exhibition venue; combined with a solo show this clearly satisfies the artistic exhibitions criterion.

criteria.name: Leading or critical role
criteria.evidence: Led design of three landmark civic buildings as principal architect
criteria.rationale: Principal architect role on multiple landmark civic projects demonstrates a leading or critical role in distinguished organizations/projects.

criteria.name: High remuneration
criteria.rationale: No salary, fees, or compensation data were described.

criteria.name: Commercial success in the arts
criteria.rationale: No box office figures, gate receipts, or equivalent commercial metrics were described.

gaps: Document any elite professional memberships (e.g., AIA College of Fellows, Royal Academy, or invitation-only architecture organizations) to address the Membership criterion
Provide specifics on peer-review judging: journal name, number of manuscripts reviewed, and any editorial board roles to strengthen Judging
Articulate a concrete original contribution — e.g., a novel structural method, design philosophy adopted by others, or citation/influence evidence — to move Original Contribution from Partial to Met
Clarify whether the monograph was peer-reviewed and/or issued by an academic press; supplement with any journal articles to strengthen Scholarly Articles
Gather compensation documentation (principal architect fee statements, salary comparisons to peers) to potentially satisfy High Remuneration
An attorney of record must review all evidence before any filing
disclaimer: This is general informational guidance only, not legal advice. Immigration law is fact-specific and changes frequently. An attorney of record licensed to practice law is required to review your petition and advise on your situation before anything is filed with USCIS.
